alternatives.name: Custom hot-rolled / cold-drawn special geometry profiles (ZOLLERN)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Near-net-shape for bogie sills, floor beams, door frames, body pillars; reduces downstream machining/fit-up. Weakness: Custom die NRE EUR 5-30K/geometry; 6-12 wk tooling lead; min batch ~100 kg; corrosion mitigation required (C10). ~40% share.

alternatives.name: Standard hot-rolled structural sections (I, U, T, H, L profiles)
alternatives.tradeoffs: No tooling lead time; commodity sourcing from ArcelorMittal, voestalpine, thyssenkrupp. Requires extensive machining/fit-up to achieve custom geometry; significant material waste vs near-net; limited profile type freedom. ~35% share.

alternatives.name: Aluminium alloy extrusions
alternatives.tradeoffs: ~60-70% weight saving vs steel; growing in passenger carbodies. Different weld process required (TIG); incompatible with standard steel carbody weld procedures; outside ZOLLERN capability (C6 material constraint); higher unit cost. ~20% share.

alternatives.name: CNC-machined parts from bar stock or plate
alternatives.tradeoffs: Maximum geometry freedom; no tooling. Very high machining cycle time; 40-80% material removal waste; cost-prohibitive at series volumes; suitable only for prototypes or single-unit specials. ~5% share.

alternatives.name: Do nothing / redesign to standard stock
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero NRE and zero lead time. Forces design team to accept geometry compromises; may increase assembly complexity, weight, or weld count to achieve equivalent structural performance.
